title: Gradualism Evolution
script_summary: Exploring gradualism evolution and its complexities.

segments.voiceover: Ever wondered how life evolves slowly, step by step?
Gradualism suggests that evolution happens in small, incremental changes over long periods.
Unlike rapid shifts or sudden appearances of new species, gradualism focuses on the slow build-up.
Fossil records often show these subtle changes—over millions of years—like the evolution of horses from small, forest-dwelling creatures.
But uncertainty looms. Not all species follow this path. Other factors like extinction and environmental shifts complicate the story.
Want to dive deeper into evolution? Subscribe for more intriguing science!
